source.title: Structural Damage - Crane Lifting Incident, Noble Stanley Lafosse

actions_taken: OIM, DSL, and STS were notified
Plan put in place to recover water bag and place crane in cradle
Announcement made warning of slick decks from hydraulic fluid
Pipe rack was barriered off to prevent slip hazards
Scupper plugs installed in drains to isolate LOPC to pipe rack
Initial inspection of brakes, motor, and crane cable completed by contractor and third party crane companies
BSEE Houma District office notified orally and written report submitted in eWell within 15 days
Onsite investigation performed by BSEE inspectors on May 16, 2024
event.summary: On May 2, 2024, during 5-year recertification load testing of the Forward Starboard knuckle boom crane (KBC #1) on the drillship Noble Stanley Lafosse, a winch brake assembly failure occurred at approximately 70 metric tons while attempting to reach the full load test of 93.5 MT. The failure resulted in uncontrolled descent of a water bag test load, causing a Loss of Primary Containment (LOPC) due to hydraulic test port failure. The water bag contacted the take-on station corner, ripped open, and descended into the Gulf of Mexico. Property damage totaled $168,150. Root cause was determined to be crane overload due to improper sea lift area designation combined with lack of personnel training on Automatic Overload Protection System (AOPS) functionality and inadequate procedures.
recommendations: Reset sea lift area when performing 5-year load test over the side of the vessel
Provide proper training on AOPS functionality to personnel performing load tests
Revise job instructions to include safety system checks before load test, not after
Improve maintenance procedures for brake overhaul and installation to ensure Quality Control checks
Update work task references and load test procedures to specify sea lift area reset requirements

threats.name: Brake assembly failure due to overload
Hydraulic motor damage from uncontrolled descent
AOPS not activated due to incorrect load parameters

consequences.name: Loss of Primary Containment - hydraulic fluid release
Water bag loss to Gulf of Mexico

controls.name: Permit to Work (PTW)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure proper authorization and planning

supporting_text: A Permit to Work (PTW), Lift Plan, and Task Risk Assessments were completed prior to starting operations.

controls.name: Lift Plan
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to define safe lifting parameters and procedures

supporting_text: A Permit to Work (PTW), Lift Plan, and Task Risk Assessments were completed prior to starting operations.

controls.name: Task Risk Assessments
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify and mitigate hazards

supporting_text: A Permit to Work (PTW), Lift Plan, and Task Risk Assessments were completed prior to starting operations.

controls.name: Automatic Overload Protection System (AOPS)
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent crane overload and control load descent
controls.lod_basis: Automatic safety system designed to safeguard against overload

supporting_text: Individuals performing the task did not have proper training on (AOPS) functionality.
There was no procedure to reset the sea lift area when performing a 5-year load test over the side of the vessel.
At 18-meter radius, with 1-meter sea state, KBC#1 should have activated AOPS and control the lowering of the load.

The lift was made over the sea without resetting the sealift area to stay in internal lift zone during testing. Resetting the sealift area will prevent the AOPS from arming / activating at SWL x dynamic factor.

controls.name: Brake assembly inspection and maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to ensure brake system integrity
controls.lod_basis: OEM requirement for 5-year recertification and brake assembly replacement

supporting_text: The load tests were being performed after the winch brake assemblies were changed, which is an Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) requirement every 5 years.
The winch brake assembly failure occurred at approximately 70 metric tons (MT) while trying to reach the full load test of 93.5 MT.
All four brake assemblies were disassembled and inspected. KBC #1 brake assembly #4 showed no signs of heat or scoring, indicating it did not disengage when AOPS was activated.
Brake overhauls are being sent to the overhaul facility individually. The brakes need to be disassembled when returned to the rig for installation. This compromised workshop Quality Control checks.

controls.name: Sprag clutch design and structural integrity
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent load loss during brake failure
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical backup to prevent uncontrolled descent

supporting_text: The structural limits of the sprag clutches were exceeded causing them to shear.

The AOPS engaged while all 70 MT was applied to the sprags / brake assembly plus the additional torque of the all 4-motors attempting to lower the load. This exceeded the structural limits of the sprag clutches and they sheared causing the uncontrolled descent of the water bag.
Sprag clutches were broken in brake assembly #4.

controls.name: Sea lift area designation and reset procedure
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure AOPS activation at correct load threshold

supporting_text: The lift was made over the sea without resetting the sealift area to stay in internal lift zone during testing.
There was no procedure to reset the sea lift area when performing a 5-year load test over the side of the vessel.
Resetting the sealift area will prevent the AOPS from arming / activating at SWL x dynamic factor.

controls.name: Work task procedures and job instructions
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure proper execution of load test procedures
controls.lod_basis: Documented procedures for safe work execution

supporting_text: Noble maintenance routine from Legacy Pacific Drilling (L-PACD) provides poor instructions on how to perform the load test and perform safety checks.
Safety checks are listed post load test.
The work task references the TEC-WWD_SOW-016 - Load Test for Post-Repair which provides poor guidance and does not specify to reset the sealift area.
Communication – Job instructions were inadequate because testing safety systems are mentioned after the 5-year load test instead of before the load test.

controls.name: Personnel training on AOPS functionality
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure personnel understand safety system operation

supporting_text: Personnel training – individuals performing the task did not have proper training on AOPS functionality.

controls.name: Incident notification and response
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to manage incident consequences and prevent escalation

supporting_text: The load test was stopped and the Offshore Installation Manager (OIM), Drill Site Leader (DSL), and Safety Training Supervisor (STS) were notified.
The situation was evaluated and a plan was put in place to recover the water bag and place the crane in cradle.
An announcement was made warning of slick decks from hydraulic fluid and the pipe rack was barriered off to prevent possible slip hazards.
Scupper plugs were installed in the drains to keep the LOPC isolated to the pipe rack.
The BSEE Houma District office was notified orally and a written report was submitted in eWell within 15 days.

controls.name: Hydraulic system pressure relief and test port design
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to limit hydraulic fluid release during system failure

supporting_text: As a result of the failed brakes, a Loss of Primary Containment (LOPC) occurred due to a hydraulic test port failure during the descent of the water bag, which sprayed hydraulic fluid onboard the rig.

The hydraulic motors were damaged due to the uncontrolled descent which caused the motors to act as pumps. LOPC occurred due to the motors becoming pumps with unregulated pressure which ejected two minimess test ports.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
